response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, some Java/C++), but the resume shows no substantive recent work in Java, Kotlin, or the Spring ecosystem, which are the core requirements for this enterprise JVM/IDE tooling role. The only IDE-adjacent experience (LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments, ~6 months in 2014-2015) was in C/C++, not JVM. There is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or JVM language-tooling work.
strengths: Prior IDE/dev-tooling exposure at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and current work on developer tooling and MCP code generators
Strong systems and large-scale codebase experience, including low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Demonstrated craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, public talks, workshops, and OSS projects
concerns: No demonstrated depth in Java or Kotlin; Java appears only as a listed skill with minimal project context
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or application-server experience mentioned anywhere
Career trajectory is centered on Node/TypeScript and DLang, not JVM ecosystem or language tooling internals

key_factors.factor: Minimal Java/Kotlin depth despite long career
